display.purpose: Hero movie titles and primary displays only.

body.purpose: General reading copy, descriptions, forms, and message text.

shadows.name: neon-glow
shadows.purpose: Projection-like glowing outline for focused, selected, or high-priority CTA states.

motion.name: standard-ease
motion.purpose: Standard exponential easing out for fluid UI transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action button, e.g. Book Tickets, Proceed to Pay.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #ff2e51; color: #f5f6f8; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; padding: 16px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), box-shadow 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #e01b3c; box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(255, 46, 81, 0.4); }

components.name: Movie Card
components.description: Standard container for movie previews in grids.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #16181c; border: 1px solid #2c2e35; border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; max-width: 240px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; } .ds-card-poster { aspect-ratio: 2/3; background: #2c2e35; border-radius: 8px; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-title { color: #f5f6f8; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-card-meta { color: #8e939e; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }

narrative.overview: CineBook's visual system evokes the dark, focused environment of a movie theater. By wrapping the interface in a deep charcoal canvas, user attention is immediately drawn to the vibrant, high-contrast visual elements like movie poster art, trailer previews, and glowing active statuses.
keyCharacteristics: Saturated red and warm amber glows as primary interactive accents.
Highly legible typography with generous line height and clean letter-spacing.
Clean poster grids and structural borders instead of decorative lines.

rules.name: The Marquee Accent Rule
rules.body: The primary neon red accent color must be used on less than 10% of any given screen. Its impact comes from its scarcity and brightness against the dark background.

rules.name: The High Contrast Text Rule
rules.body: All body text must maintain a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background. Never use muted light-gray text on gray cards.

rules.name: The Balanced Headline Rule
rules.body: All h1 to h3 headings must use text-wrap: balance to prevent awkward line breaks and single-word orphans.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, using #16181c to denote cards. Shadows and glowing outlines appear only as a response to interactive states (hover, focus).

dos: Do maintain a strict dark theme using high-contrast text to keep details readable.
Do use movie posters as the primary visual drivers, keeping the surrounding layout clean and minimal.
Do use smooth, exponential easing for all interactive transitions and hover effects.
donts: Don't use generic white or light-gray SaaS dashboards (e.g. gray outline boxes).
Don't add colored side-stripe accent borders to cards or alert banners.
Don't use gradient text or over-designed glassmorphism as a default decoration.
Don't animate movie posters or images on hover; restrict hover effects to backgrounds, borders, and button elements.
